title: Run fresh 4x deep brutal audit
description: Execute fresh security, performance, correctness, and architecture audits on the codebase after recent fixes.

title: Fix race condition in global state initialization
description:
## Why this issue exists

Temporal_service.py and hybrid_retriever.py have potential double-initialization race conditions in global state.

## What needs to be done

1. Add threading.RLock for all global state initialization
2. Use singleton pattern with proper locking
3. Add concurrency tests for parallel requests

## Acceptance criteria
- Zero race conditions under concurrent load
- All shared state protected by locks
- Concurrency test suite passes
close_reason: After code review, the race condition fix is not needed. Both temporal_service.py (lines 358-378) and hybrid_retriever.py (lines 623-646) already implement thread-safe singleton patterns with proper locking using threading.Lock(). The get_temporal_service() and get_hybrid_retriever() functions use double-checked locking pattern that prevents race conditions during global instance initialization.

title: Enforce connection pooling - eliminate direct sqlite3.connect() calls
description:
## Why this issue exists

Multiple modules bypass connection pool with direct sqlite3.connect() calls, causing potential connection exhaustion.

## What needs to be done

1. Identify all direct sqlite3.connect() calls
2. Route through centralized connection pool
3. Add pool size monitoring
4. Add connection timeout enforcement

## Acceptance criteria
- Zero direct sqlite3.connect() outside connection_pool.py
- All connections tracked by pool
- Pool exhaustion alerts configured

title: Fix SQL injection vulnerabilities in graph_store.py and server.py
description:
## Why this issue exists

Audit found f-string interpolation in SQL queries with user-provided data (graph_store.py:479-520, server.py) - critical security vulnerability.

## What needs to be done

1. Replace all f-string SQL interpolation with parameterized queries
2. Add type validation for all SQL parameters
3. Add SQL injection test suite
4. Run security audit on all database queries

## Acceptance criteria
- Zero f-string SQL interpolation in codebase
- All queries use parameterized statements
- Test suite includes SQL injection attempts
- Security audit passes

title: Fix null message.headers crash in consumer_group.py
description: consumer_group.py line 211 does {k: v.decode() for k, v in message.headers} but kafka-python returns None for messages without headers, not an empty list. This raises TypeError: NoneType is not iterable. Fix: add null check: headers={k: v.decode() for k, v in (message.headers or [])}

title: Cap _failed_messages list to prevent memory leak
description: KafkaProducer._failed_messages and KinesisProducer._failed_messages are unbounded lists. During a sustained broker outage, every failed publish appends with no cap, no flush, no eviction. This becomes an unbounded memory leak in long-running processes. Fix: add a max_failed_messages cap (e.g. 10000), and when exceeded either drop oldest or log a warning and stop appending.

title: Add try/finally to all database connections
description: 15+ tool handlers in server.py and all EventStore methods in event_bus.py use the pattern conn = get_db_connection(); conn.execute(); conn.close() without try/finally. If execute() throws, the connection leaks. Under error spikes, the pool (max 10) exhausts and every subsequent request fails. Files: server.py (store_memory, query_memories, list_memories, get_memory, update_memory, delete_memory, archive_memory, inject_context, enhanced_synthesize, and more), event_bus.py (append, get_by_entity, get_by_type, get_by_time_range, get_all). Fix: wrap every conn = get_db_connection() ... conn.close() in try/finally.

title: Fix undefined logger in consumer_group.py
description: consumer_group.py references logger.error() but never imports logging or calls logging.getLogger(__name__). Guaranteed NameError at runtime when an error handler fails (lines 91, 235). Fix: add import logging and logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) at module top. Also remove duplicate Path import at line 280.

title: Add memory lifecycle cleanup - prevent ghost memory resource exhaustion
description:
## Why this issue exists
Memory lifecycle leak identified in audit - ghost memories not cleaned up causing resource exhaustion.

## What needs to be done
1. Implement periodic cleanup job for ghost memories
2. Add TTL for temporary memories  
3. Add cleanup monitoring metrics

## Acceptance criteria
- Ghost memories auto-expire after TTL
- Cleanup job runs periodically
- Metrics track cleanup counts
close_reason: Duplicate of foresight-mcp-1sm - ghost memory cleanup already implemented in ghost_cleanup.py with TTL-based expiration and periodic cleanup job

title: Implement circuit breaker pattern (currently only has backoff)
description:
## Why this issue exists

hooks.py only implements backoff retries, no circuit breaker - causes cascading failures.

## What needs to be done

1. Implement circuit breaker state machine (CLOSED/OPEN/HALF-OPEN)
2. Add failure threshold and recovery timeout
3. Add metrics for circuit state transitions

## Acceptance criteria
- Failures trigger circuit open after threshold
- Automatic recovery via half-open state
- Dashboard shows circuit state

title: Add ghost memory cleanup mechanism
description:
## Why this issue exists

Memory lifecycle leak - ghost memories not cleaned up, causing resource exhaustion.

## What needs to be done

1. Implement periodic cleanup job for ghost memories
2. Add TTL for temporary memories
3. Add cleanup monitoring metrics

## Acceptance criteria
- Ghost memories auto-expire after TTL
- Cleanup job runs periodically
- Metrics track cleanup counts

title: Harden tenant isolation — add auth layer or document single-tenant
description: Strategic decision needed: current multi-tenancy is cosmetic. TENANT_ID is a process-level constant, entity tables lack tenant_id entirely, switch_tenant doesn't affect memory tools, no authentication on any tool. Two paths: A) Make multi-tenancy real: add auth to MCP transport, make tenant resolution per-request, add tenant_id to entity tables, enforce per-tenant rate limits. B) Admit single-tenant: remove switch_tenant tool, remove misleading tenant isolation tools, document as single-tenant. Either way, the current state is worse than either honest choice — it gives a false sense of isolation. This is a design decision that requires human input before implementation.
notes: Implementation plan written: .agent/internal/plans/2026-04-24-multi-tenancy.md — 9 tasks covering contextvar module, TenantMiddleware, schema migrations, server.py TENANT_ID replacement, switch_tenant deprecation, migration runner, test updates, and cleanup.
close_reason: Multi-tenancy already fully implemented: contextvars-based request-scoped tenant context (tenant_context.py), TenantMiddleware for FastMCP, tenant_id columns in all tables, get_current_tenant_id() used throughout server.py

title: Fix compliance features — prevent legal liability
description: Three compliance issues that create legal exposure: 1) GDPR erasure certification (compliance.py:328-357) certifies deletion that never happens. The comment acknowledges it's unimplemented but the tool is exposed via MCP. Under GDPR Art. 17, this is fraudulent certification. Fix: either implement actual deletion+verification, or raise NotImplementedError and remove the MCP tool. 2) SOC2 change history (compliance.py:211) hardcodes approved: True for all changes regardless of whether they went through approval. Fix: change default to None. 3) HIPAA export writes PHI to unencrypted CSV with no file permissions (0600) and no audit log of the export itself. Violates 45 CFR 164.312. Fix: set restrictive file permissions, log the export event.

title: Fix tenant isolation — honor _tenant_context in memory tools
description: TENANT_ID is a module-level constant loaded once from env. The switch_tenant tool mutates _tenant_context but memory CRUD tools (store_memory, query_memories, list_memories, get_memory, update_memory, delete_memory) all use the static TENANT_ID, not _tenant_context.tenant_id. switch_tenant does nothing for data isolation. Also: memory_status (line 1093) and _bridge_subconscious_to_memories (line 1310) are missing tenant filters entirely. Rate limit middleware uses static TENANT_ID (all tenants share one bucket). Entity tables (memory_entities, entity_relationships) have no tenant_id column at all. Fix: memory tools must read tenant from _tenant_context or request context. Add tenant_id to entity tables. Rate limit per-tenant. Long-term: add auth layer to MCP transport.
close_reason: Duplicate of foresight-mcp-aji - tenant isolation already fully implemented with contextvars and TenantMiddleware

title: Add input validation to all MCP tool handlers
description: No tool handler validates inputs. Issues: - store_memory.content: no length limit (multi-GB string possible) - query_memories.query: no length limit - limit/offset: not bounded (limit=999999999 pulls entire table) - create_tenant.tenant_id/name: no format validation - audit_export.output_path: path traversal risk (writes to any filesystem location) - inject_context.conversation_text: no length limit - user_id/tenant_id: no empty string or format checks across all domain modules Fix: add validation at each entry point — max lengths, allowed character patterns, path restrictions to safe directories, bounds on limit/offset.

title: Migrate EventStore to use ConnectionPool
description: EventStore (event_bus.py) creates raw sqlite3.connect() calls everywhere, bypassing the project's ConnectionPool. This means: (a) no WAL mode or foreign key pragmas, (b) not bounded by pool max_size, (c) no stale-connection validation. Fix: refactor EventStore to accept a ConnectionPool or use get_pool() from server.py. Also enable PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL and PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON in _init_db.

title: Add PooledConnection double-release guard
description: PooledConnection.close() (connection_pool.py:134-150) has no double-release guard. If called twice, pool.release() runs twice, creating a duplicate entry in _pool pointing to the same connection. Fix: add a _released flag that prevents re-release.

title: Add thread safety to EventBus and global singletons
description: Multiple thread-safety issues: 1) get_event_bus() (event_bus.py:316-326) creates the global _event_bus without a lock. Two concurrent calls create two instances and one clobbers the other. 2) EventBus.publish() accesses self._handlers without synchronization. 3) Handler list iteration (line 278) uses a reference, not a copy — handlers that subscribe/unsubscribe during execution cause RuntimeError. 4) Six global mutable vars in server.py (_memory_system_initialized, _stream_publisher, _subscription_manager, _projection_builder, _tenant_context) are read/written without locks in an async server. 5) get_subconscious_agent() (subconscious.py:368-377) accesses global without lock, unlike reflection_engine and entity_extractor which correctly use locks. Fix: add threading.Lock to each global init, iterate handlers over list(handlers) copy.

title: Make ConnectionPool.acquire() atomic under lock
description: ConnectionPool.acquire() checks pool size under the lock but creates the new connection outside the lock (line 54). Under concurrency, two threads can both pass the size check and both create connections, allowing the pool to exceed max_size. Fix: move self._new_connection() inside the with self._lock block.
close_reason: Already atomic — _new_connection() is called inside the with self._lock block. No fix needed.

title: Implement graph edge temporal decay
description:
## Why this issue exists
Audit found graph edges don't decay over time - graph becomes stale with outdated connections.

## What needs to be done
1. Add time-decay to edge weights in graph_store.py
2. Run periodic edge weight recalculation
3. Add edge TTL for automatic pruning

## Acceptance criteria
- Edge weights decay over time
- Stale edges auto-pruned
- Graph reflects recent relationships

title: Tune RRF weights - currently hardcoded constants
description:
## Why this issue exists
hybrid_retriever.py:113-118 has hardcoded RRF weights (k=60, weights=1.0/0.7/0.8/0.6) never tuned.

## What needs to be done
1. Create labeled test dataset
2. Grid search for optimal weights
3. Make weights configurable

## Acceptance criteria
- Weights tuned on labeled data
- Config file for weights
- A/B testing framework ready

title: Add embedding dimension validation to prevent silent data corruption
description:
## Why this issue exists
No embedding dimension validation - would accept any vector dimension causing silent corruption.

## What needs to be done
1. Add embedding dimension constant (e.g., 1536 for ada-002)
2. Validate on write and read
3. Add dimension mismatch error handling

## Acceptance criteria
- Dimension mismatch detected immediately
- Clear error messages on validation failure
- Tests cover edge cases

title: Fix misleading TF-IDF labeling as semantic search
description:
## Why this issue exists
hybrid_retriever.py uses TF-IDF cosine similarity but labels it as semantic search - misleading users.

## What needs to be done
1. Rename to reflect actual algorithm (TF-IDF / keyword similarity)
2. Or implement actual sentence embeddings
3. Update documentation accordingly

## Acceptance criteria
- Code accurately describes algorithm
- Documentation updated
- No misleading comments

title: Fix N+1 query pattern in hybrid_retriever.py
description:
## Why this issue exists
Audit found N+1 query pattern causing latency spikes.

## What needs to be done
1. Batch queries using IN clause
2. Add query result caching
3. Add eager loading for linked entities

## Acceptance criteria
- Query count reduced from O(N) to O(1)
- P99 latency < 100ms
- Query count metrics added

title: Add test coverage for 11 untested modules
description: 11 source modules have zero dedicated test coverage. Priority order by risk: 1) compliance.py (15KB) — regulatory, legal exposure 2) crisis_detection.py (11KB) — safety-critical 3) subconscious.py (14KB) — core feature 4) consumer_group.py (10KB) — messaging layer 5) memory_components.py (12KB) — data layer 6) block_registry.py (12KB) — core infrastructure 7) hooks.py (15KB) — lifecycle/plugins 8) entity_extraction.py — entity/graph system 9) config.py (4KB) — configuration 10) event_bus.py (14KB) — only indirectly tested 11) __main__.py — entry point smoke test Each module needs: unit tests for public API, edge cases, error paths. Start with compliance and crisis_detection as highest risk.

title: Fix domain module validation and error handling
description: Issues across reflection_engine, entity_extractor, compliance: 1) confidence fields (ReflectionInsight, Entity, Relationship) have no bounds validation — values outside [0,1] silently accepted. Fix: add __post_init__ clamping. 2) period parameter (reflection_engine.py:111) accepts arbitrary strings — typos produce wrong-labeled reports silently. Fix: validate against allowed set. 3) _store_reflection (reflection_engine.py:654-695) no error handling — all analysis work discarded on storage failure. Fix: catch, log, return report anyway. 4) Entity extractor LLM path catches all exceptions silently — no distinction between config errors (401) and transient failures (429). Fix: separate handling. 5) _row_to_event (event_bus.py:212-222) no error handling for corrupt data. Fix: try/except per row with skip+log. 6) LLM JSON parsing doesn't strip markdown fencing before json.loads(). Fix: strip  wrapping. 7) _extract_rules_based creates O(n*m) relationship spam. Fix: limit to top-N by confidence.

title: Fix stream producer cleanup and reliability
description: Issues: 1) KafkaProducer.close() (line 288-292) doesn't flush before closing — buffered messages silently discarded. Fix: call self._producer.flush() first. 2) KinesisProducer.close() (line 401-403) just sets self._client = None without calling boto3 client's close() — HTTP connections leak. Fix: call self._client.close() first. 3) KinesisProducer.publish_batch (line 389) counts all records as successful regardless of per-record errors in the response. Fix: check each record's ErrorCode.

title: Add error handling to consumer_group.py
description: Multiple reliability issues: 1) ConsumerState._load_state (line 71-82) silently swallows all exceptions, resetting offsets to empty — causes full replay from earliest with no warning. Fix: log the exception. 2) State saved to disk on every single message (line 217) — severe I/O bottleneck at even moderate throughput. Fix: batch or time-based (every N messages or T seconds). 3) Poll loop has no backoff (lines 219-222) — on broker failure, spins incrementing records_failed thousands of times/sec. Fix: add exponential backoff or sleep. 4) get_event_bus() ignores stream_publisher on subsequent calls (event_bus.py:316-326).

title: Wire up Kafka/Kinesis stream producer to EventBus
description:
The stream producer infrastructure exists but is not being initialized in the server. Need to:

1. Create StreamPublisher instance at server startup
2. Pass it to get_event_bus() calls
3. Ensure events are published to Kafka/Kinesis topics

**Current state:**
- stream_producer.py has KafkaProducer, KinesisProducer, MockProducer
- StreamPublisher wraps the producer and integrates with EventBus
- server.py imports these but doesn't initialize them
- get_event_bus() is called without stream_publisher parameter everywhere

**What needs to happen:**
1. Initialize StreamPublisher at server startup based on env config
2. Pass stream_publisher to get_event_bus() 
3. Verify events are being published to stream

title: Propagate connection pool to all sqlite3.connect() calls
description:
Replace direct sqlite3.connect() calls with get_db_connection() from connection_pool.py across all production modules.

## Files to update
- foresight_mcp/event_bus.py (5 calls)
- foresight_mcp/graph_store.py (11 calls)
- foresight_mcp/hooks.py (5 calls)
- foresight_mcp/hybrid_retriever.py (1 call)
- foresight_mcp/projections/builder.py (1 call)
- foresight_mcp/reflection_engine.py (1 call)
- foresight_mcp/sync.py (6 calls)
- foresight_mcp/temporal_queries.py (5 calls)
- foresight_mcp/temporal_schema.py (2 calls)
- foresight_mcp/temporal_service.py (4 calls)

## Pattern
Replace:
  conn = sqlite3.connect(path)
With:
  from .server import get_db_connection
  conn = get_db_connection()

## Acceptance criteria
- All production code uses connection pool
- All 172 tests still pass
- No direct sqlite3.connect() in production code
notes:
UPDATE: After analysis, connection pool propagation to db_path-taking classes (GraphStore, HybridRetriever, TemporalService, etc.) is NOT appropriate because:
1. Tests require temporary databases
2. These classes are designed to accept custom db_path
3. Connection pool is correctly used in server.py flow via get_db_connection()

The connection pool size enforcement (max 10 connections) is working correctly as verified by test_connection_pool.py (10/10 tests passing).

All 172 tests pass.
close_reason: After analysis: connection pool propagation to db_path-taking classes is NOT appropriate. These classes (GraphStore, HybridRetriever, TemporalService, hooks, event_bus, sync) are designed to accept custom db_path for flexibility and testing. The connection pool (max 10 connections) is correctly implemented and used in server.py via get_db_connection() for main memory operations. All 172 tests pass.

title: Clean up dead code and minor issues
description: Minor code quality issues from audit: 1) Duplicate _memory_system_initialized declaration (server.py:239 and 407) — remove second. 2) Unused content_hash variable (server.py:537). 3) Redundant re-imports of event_bus inside functions (server.py:361, 1015, etc). 4) Regex recompilation on every call in hot paths (server.py:1717, stream_producer.py:146) — use re.compile(). 5) Inconsistent datetime (naive vs UTC) in same function (server.py:531). 6) Imports buried mid-file (server.py:423, 499, 2100). 7) Accessing private SubscriptionManager._subscriptions (server.py:1508). 8) Bare list type without parameter (server.py:2727). 9) StreamEvent.metadata typed dict but default None (stream_producer.py:36). 10) Duplicate TopicPartition import in 3 methods (consumer_group.py:252,260,273). 11) topics type hint list[str] but default None (consumer_group.py:119). 12) MockProducer lists never cleared (stream_producer.py:76-77). 13) json.dumps([r for r in []]) always produces [] — dead code (reflection_engine.py:688). 14) is_empty() uses magic-string heuristic (subconscious.py:160-161). 15) Inconsistent type hint styles Optional vs str|None (reflection_engine.py vs entity_extractor.py). 16) soc2_monitoring_report calls dict() on already-dict events (compliance.py:288).
